sections.heading: Sector 1
sections.body:
To start your lap, you will have to navigate a short run down to Turn 1 and brake around the 100m board and shift down to 3rd gear. Clip the inside kerb of Turn 1, let the car flow into Turn 2, and be ready to swing right into Turn 3 almost immediately. This left-hander is taken in 2nd gear — dont overcook the exit. Keeping wheelspin to a minimum is key, as you power out toward a short straight. Take care not to exceed the track limits at Turn 2 — its easy to cut it slightly and invalidate your lap time.

Approach Turn 4 at full throttle, then briefly release the throttle and shift into 4th gear. Hug the inside wall. Turn 5 is more open — accelerate through it and prepare for the DRS straight ahead. This is a short burst where ERS deployment can help you gain time on the car in front dramatically.

sections.heading: Sector 2
sections.body:
Brake around the 75m board, down to 2nd gear for this 90-degree left. The corner tightens slightly on entry, so apply your braking early to avoid rear instability. This is a decent overtaking zone during the race.

A sweeping right-left-right complex where positioning matters. Turn 7 is fast — keep tight to the wall on exit. Turn 8 requires a dab of brakes and careful throttle, then a short blast into Turn 9 — a tight left-hander. Brake hard and stay off the tall inside kerb to keep balance.

Now entering the more scenic area of the circuit. Turn 10 is a 90-degree left with a tricky braking zone. You will need to rotate the car quickly in 2nd gear, then apply power early. Be wary of the bumps here — they can damage your car with relative ease or carelessness.

A narrow right into a quick left-hander (Turn 12), followed by a short straight into Turn 13. This left-hander is tight and off-camber. Brake early and be patient on the throttle. A great exit here is vital to carry your momentum into Sector 3.

sections.heading: Sector 3
sections.body:
Another 90-degree right with a wide braking zone. This is one of the better overtaking chances. Brake just after the 100m board, drop to 2nd or 3rd gear, and take a tight line. Use the kerbs sparingly to avoid upsetting the car. From here, the track becomes narrow and punishing again.

A medium-speed right-left-right combo through the underpass. These corners are tight and enclosed, so accuracy is critical. Stay off the significant sausage curbs, and manage throttle inputs carefully. Turn 17 has a tricky exit; if you release the throttle too early, you risk sliding into the wall.

One of the most unique corners in Formula One. Drive under the grandstand and over the bridge — the surface is slippery. Brake early, aim for a stable entry, and avoid running off the track. The car can feel light here due to changes in elevation and camber.

The last serious rhythm section is up next. These are right-left-right kinks that tighten up. Brake gently and use 3rd gear to rotate quickly. Wall proximity on exit can catch even the most careful drivers out. Stay focused and avoid getting greedy with the throttle.

Almost flat-out, if your balance is set up correctly, a slight lift may be needed in a race setting. Position the car well, avoid oversteering, and let the throttle open up fully on exit. Open DRS as soon as possible for the final straight.
